planning_note: DAG-validated acyclic: roots {060}; 060 -> 061 -> 063 -> 064; 060 -> 062 (conditional). CLOSED 2026-07-21 (phase 007 complete): features 060, 061, 063, 064 COMPLETE; the conditional 062 CANCELLED (the scratch-consumer pilot 063 surfaced no pre-existing root-level file collision, so the GIP Sec 5.1 rename-and-diff mechanization was not needed — carried forward as NDEBT-032 for a real brownfield consumer). Per-feature execution ledger and scope budget live in .agent/run_state.json. The pilot (063-064) was resolved against a scratch/throwaway consumer (operator option c); features 060-062 were framework-internal.

features.name: Bootstrap-stage protocol (ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md)
features.description: Author the missing Bootstrap-stage protocol document, the entry point of the canonical lifecycle. Wrap standard/GIP.md (pinned-tag clone, inject the six module dirs + NIZAM.json, GIP Sec 5 rename-and-diff + incremental adoption tiers) and bootstrap.sh; define the stage's required semantics: pinned-immutable-tag precondition (floating refs refused), payload verification + provenance drift check, the consumer-supplied inputs each ecosystem must provide (its own registry/scope/thresholds/owners/gates per NIP-0001), and the entry condition into Preflight (ecosystem/01_clean_state_preflight.md). Follow the house protocol structure matching ecosystem/01 and 02. Flip the ecosystem/README.md Module Navigation status row for 00 from Planned to Shipped; register the capability in tools/skill.json if warranted; add a CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry.
acceptance_tests: ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md exists with valid UDS frontmatter and the house protocol structure (Overview / When / Rules / Artifact / References), matching the style of ecosystem/01 and 02.

The protocol reuses and cites standard/GIP.md and bootstrap.sh rather than restating their mechanics, and states the pinned-immutable-tag precondition, the injected six-module payload, and provenance/drift verification.
ecosystem/README.md Module Navigation table marks 00 as Shipped and the surrounding prose is consistent (C10 stays green).
bash tools/validate.sh is green 15/15 (C1/C8/C9/C10 in particular), fixtures self-test passes, and tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh passes.

features.name: Define operator gate H-CONSUMER-UPGRADE
features.description: Move H-CONSUMER-UPGRADE from the RESERVED table (docs/planning/operator_gates.md Section 2) into the defined/decided set with scope (a consumer repository adopting a newly released, immutable framework tag at the Bootstrap stage), trigger condition, and disposition semantics (record-but-never-self-execute). Keep the other four reserved gates reserved. Bump the operator_gates.md registry version + change_log.
acceptance_tests: docs/planning/operator_gates.md defines H-CONSUMER-UPGRADE with scope, trigger, and disposition semantics; it is no longer listed as a reserved name only.
The four sibling gates (H-PLANNING-AUTHORITY, H-TRAIN-ENTRY, H-CONSOLIDATION, H-GA) remain RESERVED.
The gate references ecosystem/00_ecosystem_bootstrap.md as the stage it guards; registry version + change_log bumped; bash tools/validate.sh green.

features.name: (Conditional) Real-consumer bootstrap reconciliation (GIP Sec 5.1)
features.description: Implement the GIP Section 5.1 rename-and-diff behavior in bootstrap.sh so a consumer repository with a pre-existing root-level CONTEXT.md / AGENTS.md / CI configuration is never silently overwritten (today bootstrap.sh does an atomic replace of the dedicated .nizam/ target only). Add e2e coverage. CONDITIONAL: execute only if feature 063's pilot surfaces the need against a real consumer; otherwise cancelled with a recorded reason.
acceptance_tests: bootstrap.sh preserves a pre-existing root-level CONTEXT.md/AGENTS.md under a renamed path (e.g. .pre-nizam) rather than overwriting, per GIP Sec 5.1.
tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh gains a case exercising the rename-and-diff path.
If not needed, the feature is marked cancelled in feature_list_007.json with a one-line reason; bash tools/validate.sh stays green either way.
features.cancellation_reason: Not needed: the phase-007 scratch-consumer pilot (063) had no pre-existing root-level CONTEXT.md/AGENTS.md/CI collision to force GIP Sec 5.1 rename-and-diff. Deferred to a real brownfield consumer and carried as NDEBT-032 (phase-008 candidate scope).

features.name: First external-consumer pilot (handover F-016 / F-019)
features.description: Bootstrap a released framework tag (>= v0.8.0) into an operator-authorized consumer repository; run tools/validate.sh --payload inside it (must be green, proving CWD-independent payload validity in a real consumer); then drive the core loop against it: ecosystem_preflight.py --repo-root <consumer> to produce preflight.json + baseline.json, then author schema-valid findings and run ecosystem_audit.py, then compare_ecosystem_baselines.py on a second baseline/audit. Externalise evidence under the consumer's .agent/reconciliation|audits|evidence/. Record EVERY friction point as a new docs/planning/DEBT.md NDEBT-* row. Requires gate H-CONSUMER-UPGRADE (feature 061) and operator-provided consumer-repo access (product_spec_007.md Section 3).
acceptance_tests: A real, non-self consumer repository is bootstrapped against a released framework tag and tools/validate.sh --payload is green inside it.
Preflight -> Baseline -> Audit -> Compare run end-to-end against the consumer and produce schema-valid artifacts (validated via jsonschema / validate.sh --target).
Every friction point observed is recorded as an NDEBT-* row in docs/planning/DEBT.md with evidence.
The H-CONSUMER-UPGRADE gate decision for the adoption is recorded per operator_gates.md before the Bootstrap stage proceeds.

features.name: Plan next automation tranche from real friction (handover F-020) + phase close
features.description: From the pilot's recorded NDEBT friction, prioritise which of the deferred protocols (04_dependency_reconciliation, 05_release_train_coordination, 06_simplification_review, 08_ga_gate) and their companion schemas to author in a subsequent phase, evidence-driven rather than speculative. Update ROADMAP Track 4 with the pilot outcome, refresh Current Position, and close phase 007 (manifest + run_state phase_complete).
acceptance_tests: docs/planning/ROADMAP.md records the pilot outcome and a prioritised, evidence-cited candidate scope for the next phase (which deferred protocols to author and why).
docs/planning/manifest.json and .agent/run_state.json reflect phase-007 completion; bash tools/validate.sh green.
No deferred protocol (04/05/06/08) is authored without a cited pilot-evidence justification.
